title: Ohio Public Records Act Request – Employee Gift Purchasing Records (2021–2026)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Specifically, I am requesting copies of all public records regarding discretionary spending by the Superintendent and all building Principals within the district related to the purchase of gifts for employees, the selection of vendors used for these purchases, and any associated ethical disclosures. This request covers all records generated, sent, or received from January 1, 2021, through the date of this request in 2026.

The requested records include, but are not limited to:

Financial Ledgers: Expense reports, general ledger entries, and line-item statements detailing discretionary fund expenditures,

Transaction Receipts: Itemized receipts, invoices, purchase orders, and credit card statements (P-Cards) showing purchases of items intended as staff gifts,

Vendor Selection and Procurement: Bids, quotes, proposals, vendor applications, contracts, service agreements, and evaluation scorecards or notes detailing how specific businesses or vendors were selected to provide employee gifts,

Conflict of Interest & Ethics Disclosures: Copies of all conflict of interest declarations, annual financial disclosure statements, ethics training certificates, or formal disclosures submitted to or reviewed by the Ohio Ethics Commission or the Auditor of State, in which the Superintendent or any building Principal disclosed a personal, familial, or financial relationship with any vendor doing business with the district,

Communications: Emails, memos, and correspondence discussing the funding, selection, authorization, or distribution of employee gifts, or the vetting and choosing of vendors. This explicitly includes text messages (SMS, MMS, or instant messages) regarding this district business, whether conducted on district-issued communication devices or on personal, privately owned devices utilized for district operations.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide the specific legal exemption under Ohio Revised Code § 149.43 that justifies the withholding, as required by law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Margaret Jessie Carrol
